Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Normal operation; mixed objectives; moderate disruptions; policy comparison
Expected Strength: Balances welfare, sustainability, vendor viability, fulfillment, and resilience.
Expected Weakness: Requires careful weighting; poor weights may hide stakeholder-specific harms.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Multi-shock crisis; uncertain environment; long-term adaptive operation
Expected Strength: Can switch or blend policies based on scenario diagnosis and observed performance.
Expected Weakness: Harder to explain, audit, and govern; depends on reliable scenario detection.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Supply shock; vendor failure; pandemic; infrastructure outage; multi-shock crisis
Expected Strength: Maintains service continuity and reduces performance collapse under disruption.
Expected Weakness: May require redundancy, buffers, and higher cost under normal operation.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Plastic ban; carbon tax; sustainability campaign; environmental reporting
Expected Strength: Improves waste reduction, resource efficiency, and environmental outcomes.
Expected Weakness: May increase operating cost or require behavioral change.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Inflation; scholarship meal expansion; student welfare intervention
Expected Strength: Protects student access and equity during cost pressure.
Expected Weakness: May require subsidy or reduce vendor margin if not balanced.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Nutrition policy; health campaign; welfare-oriented intervention
Expected Strength: Improves health mission and average nutritional quality.
Expected Weakness: May reduce affordability or acceptance if healthy menus cost more or are less preferred.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Semester break; low demand; sustainability campaign
Expected Weakness: Too little production buffer may increase shortage risk.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: New vendor entry; vendor exit; equity-sensitive policy
Expected Strength: Improves legitimacy and balances benefit distribution.
Expected Weakness: May sacrifice short-term efficiency or high-performing stakeholder output.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Food safety incident; regulatory audit; hygiene or labeling rule change
Expected Strength: Protects safety, trust, and regulatory legitimacy.
Expected Weakness: Can constrain operational flexibility and reduce short-term performance.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Lunch peak; exam week; student complaint periods
Expected Strength: Improves student experience and may increase adoption.
Expected Weakness: Can overload popular vendors or reduce vendor fairness.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Vendor exit risk; low sales; semester break
Expected Weakness: May reduce affordability and student welfare if profit dominates.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Stable operation; low-budget condition; inflation monitoring
Expected Strength: Controls operating cost and is easy to explain.
Expected Weakness: May reduce resilience, nutrition, satisfaction, and stakeholder buffers.

Best-Fit Scenario Classes: Baseline and Online Platform reference runs
Expected Strength: Provides comparison reference for estimating value added by MSRS.
Expected Weakness: Does not coordinate stakeholder missions through recommendation vectors.
